Poultry Growing Tournament Systems: Fairness and Related Concerns

comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: If all the factors were equal, the tournament system would be great, there’s 3-4 factors that can drastically affect performance of a flock. Just to name a few The USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) recently claimed that delaying the Poultry Grower Payment Rule for 18 months would save $5.2 million in administrative costs. Let’s call that what it is: ...

When AMS finalized the Payment Systems rule, AMS explained there was uncertainty as to whether the benefits would outweigh the costs.[5] ---Comment;The current tournament pay system is expensive to run each grower is tracked in terms of bird placement, feed consumption, hen flock age, livability, % condemn, live weight, flock density, M/F ratio, avg age, chi...

I’ve been a grower in broiler industry for 11 years in that time I’ve seen the tournament as it is today only favor those who either give favors to managers of the company I grow for so that this particular ones stay on top regardless of what their true ranking in the tournament system is while honest growers suffer losses and in some cases selling out cause...

The most concerning part of this entire situation is the actual poultry grower (contract farmer) who grows the chicken, not producer (integrator) seems to have little to no input into the matter. The financial risk that the grower assumes when building or buying and operating a facility is extremely high to not have any input. To start, there is no fairness ...

I bought a poultry farm 3 years ago 6 boilers. With hopes and dreams of my family being able to run and live on a farm for generations to come. This way they could watch their kids grow and grandchildren grow. Showing that hard work and many sacrifices that was and still is being made so people can have food on there tables. But when you have an interest rat...

I’ve been seeing where the proposed grower payment has been postponed. I’m a broiler grower of almost 9 years, and through the tournament system I have ranked really well, and I have ranked really bad, all doing the same farm management flock to flock. I have seen swings of $15,000 to the bad all the while it costing me the same money to raise the bad flock ...

Delay of these badly needed rules is not necessary or appropriate. Poultry growers have been working for many years to get some much needed reform in these areas. The abusive "contract of adhesion" provisions in poultry grower contracts is wrong and needs to be illegal. Imagine that you, based on lies and false promises, put your farm and home up as collater...

comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: An overwhelming number of contract growers want this rule in place. There are too many factors that go into the tournament system that make it outdated and unfair to growers. Factors like bird and feed quality as well as timing of weather with bird placement. These factors are out of growers hands. American farm bureau has spoken to usda in face to face conv...

As a poultry farmer with decades of hands-on experience, I can tell you plainly—this delay is not just disappointing, it is deeply harmful. Growers like myself have been navigating years of rising costs, inflation, and increasing operational expenses with little relief. Labor , utilities, maintenance, and supplies have all surged, yet our compensation struct...
Oh, come on, you've had plenty of time to study this and it's time to just step up and do what's right. Don't be a pawn of the most corrupt administration the USA has ever struggled under.

The Trump administration better wake up!! We the farmers made up a huge part of the reason why He got in office in the first place. This rule that was passed 2 years ago was the only thing the poultry farmers has had done in their favor in 20 plus years, but it looks like poultry company lobbyists has written somebody a fat check to screw the American farmer...

We currently love the new rule, no longer worrying about losing a contract over feed conversion. No longer getting punished for the bad birds the company provides via a contract when we pay 40 cents for each chick but get 25 week pullets and as a result hundreds die and our bottom line suffers and they are weaker then normal chicks. The crazy thing is the br...

We have been contract poultry farmers for many years, and we were hopeful to see an end to the current tournament system. Too often, this system creates a situation where farmers are effectively competing against the company rather than being supported by it. Farmers are required to raise whatever birds are delivered to their farms, regardless of quality. Fo...

The past changes to the grower contract which required release of Breeder details (breeder age) has shed light on the iniquities in the tournament pay system. While the industry least cost their efforts; growers are not given that choice (see attached). In my opinion after 20 years as a contract grower, the integrator needs to be held responsible for action ...
I don't understand the delay "companies are all ready there Wayne-Sanderson and others"

I disagree with delaying this rule and urge implementation as soon as possible. Farmers need guide rails and clarity on what is expected without tournament style pricing. Help the farmer, help the food network in America.
The postponement is ridiculous we need something done now us poultry farmers are hurting
This is not helping the framers!! This is bull crap! USDA getting it wrong again.

I'm a poultry grower in South Central Pennsylvania. We have a fourhouse farm for an integrator, that has organic chickens in Pennsylvania. With organic chickens, chick quality and feed quality is a big deal. If you are a grower with an integrator that can give chickens medicine when they are challenged, the veterinarian and your service techs have no tools i...

All along while putting their profits in the billions on the internet for us to see each quarter just to rub it in our faces.. we haven’t had a raise in years and they’ve switched...
AS a contract poultry grower, I'm very disappointed in this decision. Just another example of being bought off by big Ag "JBS".
They have to increase the payment this in not right specially the winter time we are not making money at all to pay gas that it so please help us to pay more

Good Morning I would really like you to implement it this year and not delay any further please. Some flocks I even can’t pay my mortgage. So kindly implement it this year and do not delay any further.
Please allow the rule to be implemented as scheduled. do not delay this..we growers need protection and to be treated fairly. thankyou

Poultry growers have been wating a long time for fair pay for each grower because there are so many things they have know control over. It is a discuraging we h thought we were finally getting changes made but here we go again more delays. I hoped I would live to see changes made but I don't think It will ever happen.
